details: Combined REACT-1 and REACT-2 respondent base from the original pandemic studies; project estimate. Published follow-up frame reports 3099386 adults registered and 2494309 consented to both recontact and routine health-record linkage.
Population surveillance study measuring current SARS-CoV-2 infection in England using home PCR nose/throat swabs; 19 rounds between May 2020 and March 2022.
Adult antibody prevalence programme in England using home lateral-flow antibody testing; six rounds between June 2020 and May 2021.

Clinical measurements and biological samples in a subset of original REACT participants. Study activity ran from invitations on 06 Jan 2021 through follow-up clinic completion on 09 Dec 2021. Public pages report 10600 clinic attendees and over 10000 whole-genome analyses; project notes state the clinical cohort consented to linkage.
source_note: User-provided REACT Bio cohort plot; Imperial REACT-LC/GE; project notes

details: Online health and wellbeing survey plus cognitive assessment. Invitations were sent to 800000 REACT participants between 1 Aug and 1 Dec 2022; 276840 completed the questionnaire and 242712 were included in the published analytic cohort.
Health and wellbeing follow-up survey. The official page says about 2.5M participants were invited starting in September 2024; project notes identify a 2025 N=800k available/new-cohort subset.
